---
title: "hwpapi"
subtitle: "Pythonic automation for HWP (한컴오피스 한글)"
format:
  html:
    toc: false
    page-layout: full
---

::: {.hwpapi-hero}
# hwpapi

<p class="tagline">
A slim Python facade over HWP's COM automation — with dict-like
collections, context-managed formatting, and a documented escape hatch
to the raw action layer.
</p>

```bash
pip install hwpapi
```
:::

## 5-minute tour

```{python}
#| eval: false
from hwpapi import App

# 1. Open a file — `App` owns lifecycle only.
with App() as app:
    app.open("report.hwp")

    # 2. Everything document-scoped lives under `app.doc`.
    app.doc.insert_text("Hello, 한글!\n")

    # 3. Collections are dict-like.
    app.doc.fields["author"] = "홍길동"
    for name in app.doc.fields.names():
        print(name, "->", app.doc.fields[name].value)

    # 4. Formatting is a context scope.
    from hwpapi.context import charshape_scope
    with charshape_scope(app, bold=True, size=14, color="#C0392B"):
        app.doc.insert_text("important line\n")

    # 5. Need a raw action? Drop into `hwpapi.low`.
    from hwpapi.low import actions
    # e.g. actions._Action("MovePos")(...)

    app.save_as("report.pdf")
```

The blocks above are marked `eval: false` because they drive the HWP
COM server on Windows. Every other code block on the site that does
not need HWP runs at render time.

## What's in the box

::: {.feature-grid}

::: {.feature-card}
### Two-layer API

`App` → `app.doc` for everyday scripts. `hwpapi.low.*` for raw actions
and ParameterSet manipulation when you need it.

[Guide →](guide/app-and-document.qmd){.card-link}
:::

::: {.feature-card}
### Dict-like collections

`fields`, `bookmarks`, `hyperlinks`, `images`, `styles`, `paragraphs`,
`tables` — every collection supports `[]`, `in`, `for`, `len`, `filter`,
`names`.

[Guide →](guide/collections.qmd){.card-link}
:::

::: {.feature-card}
### Context-managed formatting

`charshape_scope`, `parashape_scope`, `styled_text` snapshot the
cursor's shape on enter and restore it on exit — even if your code
raises.

[Guide →](guide/context-scopes.qmd){.card-link}
:::

::: {.feature-card}
### Friendly units

`hwpapi.units.mm(210)`, `pt(12)`, `inch(8.27)` — no more HWPUNIT
arithmetic.

[Guide →](guide/units.qmd){.card-link}
:::

::: {.feature-card}
### Real exceptions

Every public call wraps `pywintypes.com_error` as an `HwpApiError`
subclass with a useful message.

[Reference →](reference/errors.qmd){.card-link}
:::

::: {.feature-card}
### Escape hatch, documented

`hwpapi.low.actions`, `hwpapi.low.parametersets`, `hwpapi.low.engine` —
the 900+ action wrappers and 130+ ParameterSet classes, cleanly
namespaced.

[Guide →](guide/low-level-escape-hatch.qmd){.card-link}
:::

:::

## Where to next

- **New here?** Start with [Install](getting-started/install.qmd) →
  [Quickstart](getting-started/quickstart.qmd).
- **Upgrading from v1?** The [Migration guide](getting-started/migration-v1-to-v2.qmd)
  has a 1:1 mapping table for every v1 `App` member.
- **Looking for a recipe?** See the [Recipes index](recipes/index.qmd).
- **Need to look up a symbol?** Jump to the [API Reference](reference/index.qmd).
- **Curious why it's shaped this way?** Read the
  [ADRs](design/adr/adr-001-v2-redesign.qmd).
